title: Review: address P2 review findings from seventh round
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Two real P1 API surface issues (cache_key semantics, infallible algorithm() signature) that will be painful to change after v0.1 ships. One P1 correctness bug (NotUnicode mapping). None are blocking merge but cache_key and algorithm() should be decided before publishing.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) did not read azure-kv, gcp-sm, bitwarden, doppler, wolfhsm, or aws-kms backends — they follow the same patterns but could have backend-specific error mapping gaps; (2) Vault client_error mapping covers only 404, other Vault-specific codes not checked. Would raise confidence if: read remaining backends against their SDK source, added a test that verifies EnvBackend returns DecodeFailed (not NotFound) for a non-UTF-8 env var on Linux.
